parsed_review.summary: CP00-163 adds descriptor-only Master Data synthetic fixture entry descriptors, contract v0.8 parity checks, tests, H04/C04 packets, and CP00-164 handoff. Claude found no P0/P1/P2 blockers. One P3 was fixed and one P3 was explicitly deferred as real-data/rendering hardening for later packs.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-163 builds synthetic fixture entry descriptors only; package code does not load real data, execute runtime permission checks, audit appends, API handlers, UI rendering, Hermes, or Claude.

findings.title: Denied-state safe error code is a hardcoded literal instead of the canonical source constant
findings.evidence: createMasterDataSyntheticFixtureEntryDescriptor sets the denied scenario safe_error_code to the string literal "MASTER_DATA_API_UNAUTHORIZED_OMISSION". The canonical value already exists at MASTER_DATA_PERMISSION_AUDIT_BINDING.safe_error_copy.denied (registry.js:639), and that constant is already imported into service.js. The literal currently matches exactly, so the pack is correct today, but the value is duplicated rather than referenced. If CP162's safe_error_copy vocabulary is ever revised, this descriptor would silently drift from the binding it claims to bind to. The contract does not pin the literal (synthetic_fixture_entry uses denied_state: "denied_state_safe_error_copy_only"), so there is no contract drift and no gate failure.
findings.recommended_action: Reference MASTER_DATA_PERMISSION_AUDIT_BINDING.safe_error_copy.denied instead of the literal so the denied safe error code stays bound to its canonical source.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: denied-state safe error now references MASTER_DATA_API_REFERENCE_SURFACE.error_code_taxonomy.unauthorized_omission instead of a local literal.

findings.title: Leak guard validates prohibited-category label absence, not actual content, and trusts caller-supplied refs
findings.evidence: prohibited_output_absent checks only that the prohibited *category names* (e.g. "raw_permission_rule", "audit_internal_payload", "real_client_data") are not substrings of JSON.stringify(customerFacingFixture). It does not verify that real permission rules, audit payloads, or client data are absent. customer_facing_fixture is correctly minimal (only fixture_id, scenario, ui_state, safe_error_code, permission_ref, audit_hint_ref) and the internal-only fields (tenant_id, permission_audit_binding_fixture_id, ui_surface_state_id) are kept out of the renderable surface, so within this descriptor-only, synthetic-only pack there is no actual leak. However, the builder echoes caller-supplied request.permission_ref / request.audit_hint_ref / request.safe_error_code into the customer-facing surface without sanitization, and the label-substring guard would not detect real internals injected that way. Acceptable for the synthetic-only boundary; flagged as a defense-in-depth limitation, not a present leak.
findings.recommended_action: Keep as descriptor-only assurance for this pack; if real data ever flows through this builder, replace the label-substring check with an explicit allow-list of customer-facing keys/values.
findings.post_review_disposition: deferred: content-level leak guard hardening is non-blocking for this synthetic-only descriptor pack; later real-data/rendering packs must replace label-substring checks with explicit value allow-lists.
